turns.user_query: How do I switch bands for a Switch 2 device? I’m stuck on the 2.4 band
turns.expected_assistant: To switch your Switch 2 device from the 2.4 GHz band to a different band, you'll need to adjust the Wi-Fi settings directly on the device. Look for an option to select the Wi-Fi band or network. 4. Choose the 5 GHz or 6 GHz band, if available, and connect to it. Please note that the exact steps may vary depending on the device's interface and capabilities. If your device does not support switching bands, it may be limited to the 2.4 GHz band.

turns.user_query: there is no such option. how do i split my bands manually in wifi settings?
turns.expected_assistant: To manually split your Wi-Fi bands, you can disable the Self-Organizing Network (SON) feature. This allows you to manage each band separately with its own Wi-Fi name and password. Here's how you can do it: Go to Network > Select Wi-Fi Management > Disable SON > Manage each band separately by assigning different Wi-Fi names and passwords. Disabling SON will require you to manage each network separately, and each will need its own Wi-Fi name and password.
turns.label_reason: grounded support answer; deep link 'network' resolved by assistant text

conversations.title: Why can’t I remove the password from my WiFi

turns.user_query: Why can’t I remove the password from my WiFi
turns.expected_assistant: You cannot remove the password from your Wi-Fi because Verizon routers require a security protocol to ensure network safety. The security settings available are WPA2, WPA3, or WPA2/WPA3 Mixed mode, which all require a password for access. This is a standard security measure to protect your network from unauthorized access .
turns.label_reason: grounded support answer; assistant-text BM25 oracle

turns.expected_assistant: It is not possible to bypass the requirement for a password on Verizon routers. The security protocols (WPA2, WPA3, or WPA2/WPA3 Mixed mode) are mandatory to ensure the safety and integrity of your network.
turns.label_reason: grounded support answer; assistant-text BM25 oracle

turns.expected_assistant: Even on guest networks, Verizon routers require a password for security purposes. The guest network settings include a name and password, and these cannot be removed due to mandatory encryption protocols like WPA2, WPA3, or WPA2/WPA3 Mixed mode.
turns.label_reason: grounded support answer; assistant-text BM25 oracle

conversations.title: Can't get cameras to connect

turns.expected_assistant: To troubleshoot your camera connection issues: 1. Verify that the equipment is plugged in and receiving power. 2. Power cycle the equipment by unplugging it, waiting 30 seconds, then plugging it back in. 3. Ensure the equipment is unobstructed and placed in an optimal location for signal strength. If the issue persists, you may need to use WPS (Wi-Fi Protected Setup) to connect your camera securely. To do this: Go to WPS > Toggle WPS on > Select **Start WPS** > Press the WPS button on your camera within 2 minutes. If none of these steps resolve the issue, please Call us for further assistance.
turns.label_reason: live-agent wording or tel link; route has no content page

conversations.title: My mb are slow on the game and when I ft it’s super laggy

turns.user_query: My mb are slow on the game and when I ft it’s super laggy
turns.expected_assistant: To address slow internet speeds during gaming and laggy video calls, here are some steps you can take: 1. **Run a Router Speed Test**: Go to Speed Test > Select **Start** to test the speed between your router and the internet. Review the results for download speed, upload speed, and latency. 2. **Optimize Device Priority**: If your router supports Device Priority, you can prioritize up to 2 devices for better connection speeds. Go to All Devices Page > Press and hold on a device > Activate Device Priority. 3. **Check Wi-Fi Placement**: Ensure your router is centrally located, away from physical barriers, large appliances, and electrical devices that may interfere with the signal. 4. **Troubleshoot Connection Issues**: Go to Troubleshoot > Select **Slow internet speed** for guided troubleshooting. 5. **Analyze Latency**: Use the Latency Analyzer to check connection stability to web services. Go to Network Landing Page > Advanced Tools Page > Latency Analyzer. If these steps do not resolve your issue, you may need to contact support for further assistance. You can Call us for help.
turns.label_reason: live-agent wording or tel link; route has no content page

turns.user_query: The speed test says it’s good but everything doesn’t work right
turns.expected_assistant: If your speed test results are good but your internet performance is still poor, here are some steps to troubleshoot: 1. **Check Device Health**: Go to Network > Select Wi-Fi Performance > Review Device Health. This will show the connection quality of each device, including signal strength and data rate. 2. **Analyze Latency**: Go to Network > Advanced Tools > Latency Analyzer. This tool checks connection stability to web services, which may reveal issues with external services rather than your home network. 3. **Optimize Wi-Fi Placement**: Ensure your router is centrally located, away from physical barriers and electronic devices that may interfere with the signal. 4. **Enable Device Priority**: If available, prioritize up to 2 devices for better connection speeds. Go to All Devices Page > Press and hold on a device > Activate Device Priority. 5. **Reset Wi-Fi Settings**: Go to Network > Advanced Tools > Reset Wi-Fi > Select **Reset now**. This refreshes device connections without affecting your Wi-Fi name or password. If these steps do not resolve the issue, you may need further assistance. You can Call us for support.
turns.label_reason: live-agent wording or tel link; route has no content page

turns.user_query: Reset the router and it still don’t work
turns.expected_assistant: If resetting your router did not resolve the issue, here are additional steps to troubleshoot: 1. **Check Device Health**: Go to Network > Select Wi-Fi Performance > Review Device Health. This will show the connection quality of each device, including signal strength and data rate. 2. **Analyze Latency**: Go to Network > Advanced Tools > Latency Analyzer. This tool checks connection stability to web services, which may reveal issues with external services rather than your home network. 3. **Optimize Wi-Fi Placement**: Ensure your router is centrally located, away from physical barriers and electronic devices that may interfere with the signal. 4. **Enable Device Priority**: If available, prioritize up to 2 devices for better connection speeds. Go to All Devices Page > Press and hold on a device > Activate Device Priority. 5. **Reset Wi-Fi Settings**: Go to Network > Advanced Tools > Reset Wi-Fi > Select **Reset now**. This refreshes device connections without affecting your Wi-Fi name or password. If these steps do not resolve the issue, you may need further assistance. You can Call us for support.
turns.label_reason: live-agent wording or tel link; route has no content page

conversations.title: I need help with extender tried everything

turns.expected_assistant: If your extender is not showing in the Equipment Page, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the extender is powered on and connected to the network. 2. Restart the extender by unplugging it, waiting 30 seconds, and plugging it back in. 3. Verify that the extender is within range of the router or gateway. 4. Check if the extender's LED indicators show a connection status. If the extender still does not appear in the Equipment Page, you may need to reset it: Go to Equipment > Select your extender > Select **Restore or Reset** > Follow the reset instructions. If the extender remains undetected, please Call us for further support.
turns.label_reason: live-agent wording or tel link; route has no content page

turns.expected_assistant: If your extender is not showing in the Equipment section despite troubleshooting, it may not be properly connected to your network. Try these steps: 1. Ensure the extender is powered on and the LED indicators show activity. 2. Move the extender closer to the router or gateway to ensure it is within range. 3. Restart the extender by unplugging it, waiting 30 seconds, and plugging it back in. 4. Attempt to reconnect the extender to your network using the WPS button: - Press the WPS button on your router. - Within 2 minutes, press the WPS button on your extender. - Wait for the extender's LED to indicate a successful connection. If the extender still does not appear in the Equipment section, it may need to be set up again. Go to Equipment > Select **Add Extender** > Follow the setup instructions. If the issue persists, please Call us for further assistance.
turns.label_reason: live-agent wording or tel link; route has no content page

Attempt to reconnect the extender using WPS: - Go to WPS > Select **Start WPS** > Press the WPS button on the extender within 2 minutes. conversations.title: How do i block any new devices

turns.user_query: How do i block any new devices
turns.expected_assistant: To block new devices from accessing your network, you can use the Parental Controls feature to manage internet access. Here's how: Go to Devices > Select the device you want to block > Pause Internet. Alternatively, you can create a profile in Parental Controls to manage multiple devices: Go to Home Page > Select Parental Controls > Add profiles > Assign devices to the profile > Pause Internet for the profile. This feature blocks new online activity and newly loaded content from accessing the internet. However, content already downloaded or buffered may still work.
turns.label_reason: grounded support answer; deep link 'tab-devices' resolved by assistant text

turns.user_query: My son is changing his pc mac address to bypass parental controls. What can i do
turns.expected_assistant: To prevent your son from bypassing Parental Controls by changing his PC's MAC address, you can take the following steps: 1. Use the **Pause Internet** feature to block new online activity for the profile associated with his devices. This will prevent newly loaded content from accessing the internet, even if the MAC address changes. Go to Home Page > Select Parental Controls > Select the profile > Select **Pause Internet**. 2. Set up **Filter Content** to block specific categories, apps, or websites. This ensures that even if the MAC address changes, the content restrictions remain in place. Go to Home Page > Select Parental Controls > Select the profile > Select **Filter Content** > Choose categories or manually enter URLs to block. 3. Use **Set Schedules** to restrict internet access during specific times. This limits the ability to bypass controls by changing the MAC address. Go to Home Page > Select Parental Controls > Select the profile > Select **Set Schedules** > Add or edit schedules. 4. Monitor the **Usage History** to identify unusual activity or new devices attempting to connect. Go to Home Page > Select Parental Controls > Select the profile > Select **View Usage**. If these measures do not resolve the issue, you may need to contact Verizon support for advanced security options.
turns.label_reason: grounded support answer; assistant-text BM25 oracle

turns.user_query: I need the time controls to work. He bypasses them when he changes mac address
turns.expected_assistant: To ensure time controls work effectively and prevent bypassing through MAC address changes, you can take the following steps: 1. **Pause Internet for the Profile**: This will block all new online activity for devices in the profile, regardless of MAC address changes. Go to Home Page > Select Parental Controls > Select the profile > Select **Pause Internet**. 2. **Set Schedules**: Restrict internet access during specific times by setting schedules for the profile. Go to Home Page > Select Parental Controls > Select the profile > Select **Set Schedules** > Add or edit schedules. 3. **Monitor Usage History**: Regularly check the usage history to identify unusual activity or new devices attempting to connect. Go to Home Page > Select Parental Controls > Select the profile > Select **View Usage**. 4. **Assign Devices to Profiles**: Ensure all known devices are assigned to the profile. This limits the ability to bypass controls by changing MAC addresses. Go to Home Page > Select Parental Controls > Select the profile > Select **Manage Devices** > Add or remove devices. If these measures do not resolve the issue, you may need to contact Verizon support for advanced security options.
turns.label_reason: grounded support answer; assistant-text BM25 oracle
